title: Identification of a Plasmid: Transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bkn0f","text":"Protocol for the identification of a plasmid by using transformation of E. Coli","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Transfer 1 mL E. Coli to each of two 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube
Centrifuge at full speed for 00:00:30 , then remove the supernatant
Add 450 µL of TFB1 to each tube, then resuspend gently by pipetting up and down (do not vortex) Keep everything On ice Incubate on ice for 00:10:00
Pellet the bacteria by centrifugation at full speed for 00:00:30 Remove the supernatant Resuspend the pellets in 100 µL of TFB2 Incubate on On ice for 00:10:00
Add 100 ng of plasmid DNA to the competent cells, and mix gently
Heat shock the cells by transferring directly from the ice to the 37 °C hot block for 00:05:00 Then incubate on ice for 00:05:00 , and add 1 mL LB broth to each tube
Incubate again at 37 °C , for 00:30:00 , and allow the cells to recover
Pellet the bacteria again by centrifuging at full speed for 00:00:30 Discard the supernatant
Resuspend the pellet in 400 µL of LB broth
Spread 100 µL of cells per plate, onto the appropriate antibiotic plates
creator: SGD
